title: Vulca Product Launch Deck
audience: builders, designers, AI platform reviewers, and early technical adopters

slides.title: Vulca
slides.claim: Design knowledge becomes editable presentation code.
slides.proof_object: A simple system mark showing case pack, skill, code, review, and deck output.

slides.title: Fast decks are not the same as designed decks
slides.claim: Prompt-only presentation tools collapse design intent into generic templates.
slides.proof_object: Three failure modes: weak hierarchy, opaque design logic, fragile editability.

slides.title: The next presentation system needs design memory
slides.claim: The opportunity is not faster slide generation; it is reusable design reasoning.
slides.proof_object: Before/after shift from prompt-to-deck to case-pack-to-editable-code.

slides.title: Vulca turns references into a workflow
slides.claim: Public commercial references become structured rules before any slide is generated.
slides.proof_object: Pipeline diagram: sources, summaries, rules, slide patterns, style tokens, generation brief.

slides.title: The product is a control layer
slides.claim: Vulca controls story, visual system, assets, code generation, and review.
slides.proof_object: Five pillars with one proof label each.

slides.title: Case packs make taste inspectable
slides.claim: A case pack stores design decisions as files that agents and humans can review.
slides.proof_object: File-to-decision map linking each case-pack artifact to a deck decision.

slides.title: Generation is code-first
slides.claim: The deck is built from editable text, shapes, SVGs, and diagrams, with images as support.
slides.proof_object: Artifact grid showing PPTX, screenshots, layout JSON, and review notes.

slides.title: The benchmark is baseline versus Vulca
slides.claim: The case-pack deck should beat an ordinary prompt deck on story, hierarchy, coherence, and editability.
slides.proof_object: Comparison matrix with the rubric dimensions.

slides.title: The first wedge becomes product primitives
slides.claim: Evidence from this deck decides whether to productize the skill, schema, layout engine, or review loop.
slides.proof_object: Three-stage path: case pack, reliable slide primitives, reusable PPT workflow.

slides.title: Design work should leave evidence
slides.claim: Vulca makes presentation design reusable, reviewable, and editable.
slides.proof_object: Final call to run the workflow on the next real commercial deck.
